name: Honcho
pricing_notes: Free tier available; paid plans for higher usage; API usage-based

auth.notes: HONCHO_API_KEY; app ID + user session management

performance.notes: Memory retrieval and storage; Bloom Filter for efficient session lookup

agent_readiness.notes: User-centric memory and personalization layer for AI apps. Stores user facts, preferences, and conversation history. 'Dialectic' inference for user modeling. LangChain integration. Designed for multi-session user personalization in production apps.

gotchas: Early stage (0.x) — API stability not guaranteed
Smaller community vs Mem0 or Zep
Documentation gaps for advanced use cases
User modeling ('Dialectic') accuracy depends on sufficient interaction history
best_when: Building personalized AI applications needing user-level memory persistence
Multi-session agents that need to remember user preferences across conversations
Apps where personalization improves UX over time
LangChain-based agents needing user memory layer
avoid_when: Simple single-session chatbots without personalization needs
Production systems requiring mature, stable APIs
Non-LangChain stacks without time to build integration
summary: Honcho provides a user-centric memory and personalization layer for AI apps. Stores user facts and conversation history across sessions, with 'Dialectic' inference for building user models. LangChain integration. Best for apps where remembering individual user preferences across sessions improves experience. Early-stage competitor to Mem0 and Zep.
